summary: Task 4 is a clean, behavior-preserving extraction per D22. Inline List<Content> assembly was moved verbatim into a pure `MultimodalContentsBuilder.build` in :core-runtime/common/, order (images -> audio -> text) and empty-text guard are identical to the original, and LlmChatModelHelper.runInference now delegates via named arguments. Imports are correctly pruned, the Bitmap.toPngByteArray extension was moved along with its usage, Robolectric 4.12 is wired as testImpl as specified by D20, and four tests cover the contract. Zero critical or major findings.

suggestions.suggestion: Replace `text.trim().isNotEmpty()` with the idiomatic `text.isNotBlank()` to avoid allocating a trimmed copy of the string on every call. Semantically identical for this contract (both use Character.isWhitespace via the default predicate) and preserves D22's 'behavior unchanged' guarantee.
suggestions.benefit: Removes a per-call String allocation, improves readability, aligns with Kotlin stdlib idioms used elsewhere in the module.

suggestions.suggestion: PNG encoding ignores the quality argument (PNG is lossless), so `compress(PNG, 100, stream)` is slightly misleading. Consider a short comment noting the quality is a no-op for PNG, or pass `0`/`1` — the litert-lm payload is byte-identical either way. Pre-existing from Gallery; only worth cleaning up because the helper is now a dedicated, isolated unit.
suggestions.benefit: Removes a common source of confusion when readers assume the 100 is meaningful; signals the lossless intent explicitly.

suggestions.suggestion: The `@Config(sdk = [33])` pin is per-class. Once a second Robolectric test lands in :core-runtime (e.g., MediaUtilsTest in Task 3 / Wave 2), consider extracting the SDK level to a shared base class or a `robolectric.properties` file under `src/test/resources` so all Robolectric tests track the same target SDK. Not needed yet — acceptable as-is.
suggestions.benefit: Keeps future Robolectric tests consistent and avoids drift between per-class SDK pins.
